This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - **Shape:** Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - **Dimensions:** - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - **Construction Features:** - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - **1928-1929:** Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - **1973, 1975:** Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - **1994:** Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - **2002:** National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - **Shape:** Irregular rounded square. - **Construction Features:** - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun-qq]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts

The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | Alternate name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | Latitude = 36.750069 | Longitude = 140.133022 | ISO = JP-09 | Shape = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | Size = Mound length 50 m | Burial Facility = Charcoal chamber | Artifacts = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | Remarks = | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Zenpo-kohofun-South | Label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Tumulus) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Tumulus) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits artifacts from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Shrine Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is designated as a National Historic Site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics - '''Shape:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. - '''Dimensions:''' - Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. - '''Construction Features:''' - No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. - Artifacts such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] - Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. - Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. - A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] Artifacts recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === Construction Period The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was designated a National Historic Site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == - '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and artifacts (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] - '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] - '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] - '''2002:''' National Historic Site designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|Artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like platform, and artifacts such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated artifacts. - '''Shape:''' Irregular rounded square. - '''Construction Features:''' - Partially paved base. - Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] - Artifacts: Haji ware jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was designated a National Historic Site in 2002. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of
