Shrine Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 2002. == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. (part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 2002. == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = National {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = National {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = National {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | other_designation = National {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is other_designation1 as a National other_designation. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National other_designation other_designation.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 2002. == other_designation == === National other_designation === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit Structure == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == (Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''Construction Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''Construction Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods
