{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 吉田温泉神社古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後方部（削平）、右奥に前方部） |別名 = 温泉神社1号墳 |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]]
（吉田新宿古墳群/温泉神社古墳群） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田・小川 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 45|緯度秒 = 0.25 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 8|経度秒 = 2.88 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長50m |埋葬施設 = 木炭槨 |出土品 = 鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「吉田温泉神社古墳群」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 吉田温泉
神社古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''吉田温泉神社古墳'''（よしだゆぜんじんじゃこふん、温泉神社1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田・小川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]（うち吉田新宿古墳群・温泉神社古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 本項では、吉田温泉神社の南にある'''[[#観音堂古墳|観音堂古墳]]'''（温泉神社21号墳）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==

小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||'''吉田温泉神社古墳'''||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
[[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|温泉神社（前方部正面）}}]] 栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘端部に築造された古墳である。[[前方後方墳]]2基・[[方墳]]20基以上からなる吉田新宿古墳群の北部に位置する{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在までに後方部が削平されているほか、前方部墳頂には温泉神社が鎮座する。[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]47年）・[[1975年]]（昭和49年）・[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。後方部は削平されているが、墳丘長は50メートル弱と見積もられる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]は認められず、墳丘盛土最下層からは甕・器台・埦が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。墳丘周囲には削り出しによるテラスを有し、その外側には幅9-12メートル・深さ約1.5メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝内からは赤彩の有段口辺壺が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。また旧表土上面には焼土・炭化物が認められ、墳丘築造前の祭祀行為が想定されるほか{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}、墳丘北西隅付近では一辺約8メートルを測る竪穴状遺構が検出されている（古墳埋葬に先立つ葬送儀礼である[[殯]]の場か）{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。後方部削平の際に木炭が出土しており、埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨と想定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。また削平の際、副葬品として鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・[[土師器]]が出土している{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、遅くとも[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉-[[5世紀]]初頭（または4世紀中葉{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。墳丘主軸を南北軸とする点で特徴を示し、かつては駒形大塚古墳・那須八幡塚古墳（東西軸）から上侍塚古墳・上侍塚北古墳・下侍塚古墳（南北軸）への過渡期を示すと考えられたが、近年では木炭槨・無葺石・有段口辺壺の点で駒形大塚古墳に後続し那須八幡塚古墳に先行する時期に想定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[2002年]]（平成14年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1928年|1928]]-[[1929年]]（[[昭和]]3-4年）、後方部墳丘の削平。木炭・鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・土師器の出土（短冊形鉄斧・土師器のみ現存）{{Sfn|温泉神社古墳（平凡社）|1988}}史跡説明板。。 * [[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）、墳形確認の発掘調査{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、墳形確認の発掘調査{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 * [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）、墳形確認の発掘調査（小川町教育委員会）{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 竪穴状遺構 == 吉田温泉神社古墳の墳丘北西隅付近では、竪穴状遺構が検出されている。遺構は一辺約8メートルを測り、東壁には高さ約25センチメートルの祭壇状の高まりを伴い、壇上からは土師器（器台・埦・高坏・鉢など）が並んだ状態で検出されている{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。特殊な遺構であり、古墳埋葬に先立つ葬送儀礼である[[殯]]との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。 == 観音堂古墳 == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|観音堂古墳}}]] '''観音堂古墳'''（かんのんどうこふん、温泉神社21号墳）は、吉田温泉神社古墳の南にある古墳。形状は[[方墳]]。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）・[[1991年]]（平成3年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は不整形な隅丸方形で、墳丘長は約31メートルを測る観音堂古墳説明板（那珂川町設置）。。墳丘裾部の一部では敷石が認められる。墳丘周囲には不整形の周溝が巡らされ、幅は広い南側で9メートルを測り、周溝内からは土師器（器台・壺）が検出されている。築造時期は古墳時代前期頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1972年]]（昭和47年）に小川町指定史跡に指定され、2002年（平成14年）に国の史跡に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 吉田温泉神社古墳群（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 吉田温泉神社古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（那珂川町教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=温泉神社古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|温泉神社古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=2002|chapter=吉田温泉神社古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=|title=吉田温泉神社古墳 墳形確認調査報告|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=吉田新宿古墳群|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[那須小川古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}} * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html 那須小川古墳群] - 那珂川町ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=観音堂古墳 (那珂川町) |1-1=栃木県の古墳 |1-2=那珂川町の歴史 |1-3=方墳 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:よしたゆせんしんしやこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:那珂川町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 吉田温泉神社古墳 |画像 = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後方部（削平）、右奥に前方部） |別名 = 温泉神社1号墳 |所属 = [[那須小川古墳群]]
（吉田新宿古墳群/温泉神社古墳群） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田・小川 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 45|緯度秒 = 0.25 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 8|経度秒 = 2.88 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長50m |埋葬施設 = 木炭槨 |出土品 = 鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「吉田温泉神社古墳群」
（「[[那須小川古墳群]]」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 吉田温泉
神社古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''吉田温泉神社古墳'''（よしだゆぜんじんじゃこふん、温泉神社1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[那須郡]][[那珂川町]]吉田・小川にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[那須小川古墳群]]（うち吉田新宿古墳群・温泉神社古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 本項では、吉田温泉神社の南にある'''[[#観音堂古墳|観音堂古墳]]'''（温泉神社21号墳）についても解説する。 == 概要 ==

神社||'''吉田温泉神社古墳'''||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
[[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|温泉神社（前方部正面）}}]] 栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘端部に築造された古墳である。[[前方後方墳]]2基・[[方墳]]20基以上からなる吉田新宿古墳群の北部に位置する{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在までに後方部が削平されているほか、前方部墳頂には温泉神社が鎮座する。[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]47年）・[[1975年]]（昭和49年）・[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。後方部は削平されているが、墳丘長は50メートル弱と見積もられる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]は認められず、墳丘盛土最下層からは甕・器台・埦が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。墳丘周囲には削り出しによるテラスを有し、その外側には幅9-12メートル・深さ約1.5メートルの周溝が巡らされ、周溝内からは赤彩の有段口辺壺が検出されている{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。また旧表土上面には焼土・炭化物が認められ、墳丘築造前の祭祀行為が想定されるほか{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}、墳丘北西隅付近では一辺約8メートルを測る竪穴状遺構が検出されている（古墳埋葬に先立つ葬送儀礼である[[殯]]の場か）{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。後方部削平の際に木炭が出土しており、埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における木炭槨と想定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。また削平の際、副葬品として鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・[[土師器]]が出土している{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、遅くとも[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉-[[5世紀]]初頭（または4世紀中葉{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。墳丘主軸を南北軸とする点で特徴を示し、かつては駒形大塚古墳・那須八幡塚古墳（東西軸）から上侍塚古墳・上侍塚北古墳・下侍塚古墳（南北軸）への過渡期を示すと考えられたが、近年では木炭槨・無葺石・有段口辺壺の点で駒形大塚古墳に後続し那須八幡塚古墳に先行する時期に想定される{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 古墳域は[[2002年]]（平成14年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1928年|1928]]-[[1929年]]（[[昭和]]3-4年）、後方部墳丘の削平。木炭・鉄剣・短冊形鉄斧・土師器の出土（短冊形鉄斧・土師器のみ現存）{{Sfn|温泉神社古墳（平凡社）|1988}}史跡説明板。。 * [[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）、墳形確認の発掘調査{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 * [[1975年]]（昭和50年）、墳形確認の発掘調査{{Sfn|那須小川古墳群|2003}}。 * [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）、墳形確認の発掘調査（小川町教育委員会）{{Sfn|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[2002年]]（平成14年）12月19日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち）。 File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む ファイル:吉田温泉神社古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館展示。}} == 竪穴状遺構 == 吉田温泉神社古墳の墳丘北西隅付近では、竪穴状遺構が検出されている。遺構は一辺約8メートルを測り、東壁には高さ約25センチメートルの祭壇状の高まりを伴い、壇上からは土師器（器台・埦・高坏・鉢など）が並んだ状態で検出されている{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。特殊な遺構であり、古墳埋葬に先立つ葬送儀礼である[[殯]]との関連を指摘する説が挙げられている{{Sfn|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}。 == 観音堂古墳 == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|観音堂古墳}}]] '''観音堂古墳'''（かんのんどうこふん、温泉神社21号墳）は、吉田温泉神社古墳の南にある古墳。形状は[[方墳]]。[[1981年]]（昭和56年）・[[1991年]]（平成3年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は不整形な隅丸方形で、墳丘長は約31メートルを測る観音堂古墳説明板（那珂川町設置）。。墳丘裾部の一部では敷石が認められる。墳丘周囲には不整形の周溝が巡らされ、幅は広い南側で9メートルを測り、周溝内からは土師器（器台・壺）が検出されている。築造時期は古墳時代前期頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[1972年]]（昭和47年）に小川町指定史跡に指定され、2002年（平成14年）に国の史跡に指定されている（史跡「那須小川古墳群」のうち）。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 吉田温泉神社古墳群（史跡「[[那須小川古墳群]]」のうち） *: 2002年（平成14年）12月19日、既指定の史跡「駒形大塚古墳」に吉田温泉神社古墳群・那須八幡塚古墳群を追加指定して、指定名称を「那須小川古墳群」に変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|492|那須小川古墳群}}。 == 関連施設 == * 那珂川町なす風土記の丘資料館（那珂川町小川） - 吉田温泉神社古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（那珂川町教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|title= 国指定史跡 那須小川古墳群（小川町文化財資料集 第12冊）|publisher=小川町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群|2003}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=温泉神社古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|温泉神社古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=2002|chapter=吉田温泉神社古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|吉田温泉神社古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E5%B0%8F%E5%B7%9D%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444653 那須小川古墳群]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|那須小川古墳群（国指定史跡）}}}} - (part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == - Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{citation needed|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|吉田温泉神社古墳|version=99994946|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|吉田温泉神社古墳|version=99994946|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun | image = [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: flattened rear section, back right: front section) | alternate_name = Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun | Part Of = {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}}
(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu District, Tochigi|qq=}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}} Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic sites of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} Kofun located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannondo Kofun|qq=}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|ja|那須小川古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|吉田温泉神社古墳|version=99994946|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

(Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group/Onsen Shrine Kofun Group) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]] Yoshida/Ogawa | coordinates = 36.750069 | coordinates = 140.133022 | omitted = JP-09 | type = {{ill|Zenpo-kohofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo-kohofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length 50 m | burial = Charcoal chamber | burial goods = Iron sword, rectangular iron axe, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Date = 4th century | Deity = | Mausoleum = | Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group"
(part of "Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group") | Tangible designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Zenpo-kohofun-South | map_dot_label = Yoshida Onsen
Shrine Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun''' (吉田温泉神社古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} Kofun located in [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]], specifically the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, and is Historic site as a National Historic site. This article also covers the nearby '''{{ill|Kannondo Kofun|ja|観音堂古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannondo Kofun|qq=}}''' (Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == Overview == [[File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Onsen Shrine (front section)}]]] The Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun is located on a terrace at the edge of the Nakagawa River's right bank in northeastern Tochigi Prefecture. It lies in the northern part of the Yoshida Shinjuku Kofun Group, which includes two zenpo-kohofun and over 20 square-shaped tumuli. The rear section of the mound has been flattened, and the Onsen Shrine is now situated atop the front section. Excavations were conducted in 1973, 1975, and 1994.[1] === Characteristics * '''type:''' Rectangular front-rear mound (zenpo-kohofun), oriented south. * '''area:''' ** Estimated mound length: slightly less than 50 meters. * '''built Features:''' ** No fukiishi (stone covering) detected. ** burial goods such as jars, pedestals, and bowls were found at the base of the mound.[2] ** Surrounding moat: 9-12 meters wide and 1.5 meters deep. ** Ritual evidence: Burnt soil and charred remains suggest pre-construction rituals. ** A vertical pit structure, 8 meters square, was identified in the northwest corner, possibly used for funerary rites.[3] burial goods recovered include an iron sword, a rectangular iron axe, and haji ware pottery. The burial facility at the rear section is thought to have been a charcoal chamber.[4] === built The tumulus is estimated to have been built around the late 4th century to early 5th century, or mid-4th century according to some interpretations.[5] The tumulus represents a transitional period between earlier tumuli with east-west orientations (e.g., Komagata Otsuka Kofun) and later north-south orientations (e.g., Kamijizuka Kofun).[6] The site was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002 as part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group. == History == * '''1928-1929:''' Rear section flattened, and burial goods (charcoal, iron sword, rectangular iron axe, haji ware) were unearthed.[7] * '''1973, 1975:''' Excavations to confirm mound structure.[8] * '''1994:''' Further structural investigations conducted by the Ogawa Town Board of Education.[9] * '''2002:''' National Historic site Historic site.[10] File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front to the rear section File:Yoshida Yuzenjinja Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear to the front section File:吉田温泉神社古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods
{{small|Exhibited at Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum.}} == Vertical Pit burial == The vertical pit structure at the northwest corner of the mound measures 8 meters per side. The eastern wall includes a 25 cm high altar-like plattype, and burial goods such as haji ware pedestals, bowls, and jars were found on it. This structure is believed to have been used for funerary rites preceding burial.[11] == Kannondo Kofun == [[File:Kannondou Kofun (Nakagawa, Tochigi), funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kannondo Kofun}]]] The '''Kannondo Kofun''' (観音堂古墳, Onsen Shrine No. 21 Kofun) lies south of Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. It is an irregularly rounded square tumulus, 31 meters in length. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 revealed its structure and associated burial goods. * '''type:''' Irregular rounded square. * '''built Features:''' ** Partially paved base. ** Surrounding moat up to 9 meters wide.[12] ** burial goods: Haji pottery jars and pedestals. The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|吉田温泉神社古墳|version=99994946|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]]

The tumulus dates to the early Kofun period and was Historic site a National Historic site in 2002. == Historic site == === National Historic site === - Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group (part of the Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group)[13] == Related Facilities == - Nasukawa Furusato-no-Oka Museum (Nakagawa, Tochigi): Exhibits burial goods from Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Related Articles == - [[:en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group]] == External Links == - [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Group] (Nakagawa Town Official Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|吉田温泉神社古墳|version=99994946|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[en:Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:吉田温泉神社古墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]]
